steps.title: Identify the contrast and the evidence
steps.content:
The passage contrasts an uncertain initial attitude (“Whatever the general attitude... when they were initially released”) with the current view (“now tend to be regarded quite ______”).

The 2018 evidence follows immediately: critics praised the films as “galvanizing” and “exuberant”—clearly positive, admiring language.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the positive evidence
steps.content:
”Favorably” directly captures positive regard, aligning with the enthusiastic praise from the New York Times critics.

Skeptically, neutrally, and strangely each suggest doubt, indifference, or oddness—none of which fit the favorable language shown in the evidence.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
